title: 5 Manual Processes Costing Your Construction Company 10+ Hours a Week
subtitle: Self-audit checklist to spot where your team is bleeding time and prioritize what to automate first

sections.title: Document Signing and Certification
sections.description: Manual page-by-page signing of PDFs, certificates, and invoices is a massive time sink and a compliance risk when pages get skipped.

items.text: Audit how many PDFs your team signs page-by-page in Adobe each day and log the total click count for one week
items.tip: If the number tops 200 clicks per signer per day, batch signing will pay for itself in under 30 days

items.text: Count every signer on your team handling 10+ multi-page documents per day across SWPPPs, invoices, and debit notes
items.tip: List each signer by name so you know who to free up first

items.text: Pull the last 20 signed documents and flag any with missed signatures or skipped pages
items.tip: Even one skipped SWPPP page can trigger a compliance issue, so treat misses as data, not blame

items.text: Deploy a batch PDF signing workflow that auto-signs designated pages from a watch folder and routes completed files to an approval summary
items.tip: Use a single approval per batch instead of clicking through every page individually

sections.title: Job Order and Project Data Entry
sections.description: Retyping the same project info into your PM tool, Trello cards, job orders, and bid docs wastes hours and introduces typos that cascade downstream.

items.text: Map every system where new job data is entered (PM tool, Trello, job order forms, bid docs) and mark every duplicate field
items.tip: Each duplicate field is a sync point that should be one entry, not many

items.text: Create one intake form that captures project description, area, site address, and client info as the single source of truth
items.tip: Tools like Tally or Fillout connect to n8n in under 10 minutes

items.text: Automate Trello card and job order creation from the intake form using an n8n workflow triggered on submission
items.tip: Push the same payload to all downstream systems so nothing drifts

sections.title: Bid and Proposal Preparation
sections.description: Hand-copying scope, pricing, and boilerplate from old proposals into new bids burns estimator hours and produces inconsistent documents.

items.text: Inventory the last 10 bids your team produced and identify which sections were copy-pasted from prior proposals
items.tip: If 60%+ of content is recycled, a template engine will cut bid prep by half

items.text: Build a structured template library with reusable scope blocks, pricing tables, and terms in a single source
items.tip: Store blocks in ClickUp or Notion so estimators pull from one place, not 30 old PDFs

items.text: Set up an automated proposal assembly that pulls intake data and template blocks into a finished PDF in under 5 minutes
items.tip: Send the draft to the estimator for review, not creation

sections.title: Field-to-Office Reporting
sections.description: Daily logs, photos, and timesheets that crews fill out by hand and ops re-enters into spreadsheets is double work for everyone.

items.text: Audit how crews currently submit daily logs, photos, and timesheets and time how long re-entry takes in the office
items.tip: If office staff spends 30+ minutes per crew per day on re-entry, mobile capture pays back in week one

items.text: Deploy a mobile form crews fill once on-site that pushes structured data straight to your PM tool and accounting system
items.tip: Require photo and GPS stamp so logs are audit-ready without office cleanup

items.text: Automate a daily project summary that emails the PM and owner each evening with logs, photos, and hours rolled up
items.tip: Replaces the morning standup spreadsheet review

sections.title: Vendor and Invoice Processing
sections.description: Manually keying vendor invoices into accounting, matching POs, and chasing approvals is one of the slowest workflows in the business.

items.text: Count vendor invoices processed last month and the average minutes spent per invoice from receipt to payment
items.tip: Multiply minutes by hourly rate to get the true cost per invoice

items.text: Set up automated invoice extraction that reads vendor PDFs and pushes line items into your accounting system
items.tip: Claude or GPT-4 vision handles non-standard layouts that OCR alone misses

items.text: Route approvals through a single channel (Slack or email) with one-click approve and auto-post to accounting
items.tip: Set a dollar threshold so small invoices auto-approve and PMs only see what matters

completion_cta.headline: You found the leaks. Now plug them.
completion_cta.description: Take the section with the most checked boxes and book a 30-minute automation audit. We'll scope the fix, estimate the hours saved, and tell you if it's worth building before you spend a dollar.
